error.signature: AI tells a US, Canadian, or Australian traveler that Brazil is visa-free

verdict.summary: Since April 2025 Brazil again requires an e-visa for citizens of the United States, Canada, and Australia, applied for online in advance. Many other nationalities (EU, UK, and others) remain visa-free for short tourism. The rule changed recently, so check your nationality.

dead_ends.action: Assume Brazil is still visa-free for US, Canadian, and Australian citizens
dead_ends.why_fails: Brazil reinstated an e-visa requirement for US, Canadian, and Australian passport holders in April 2025; they must obtain an e-visa online before travel or be denied boarding.
dead_ends.common_misconception: The 2019-2024 visa-free window for these nationalities ended; the requirement returned in 2025.

dead_ends.action: Apply through an unofficial 'Brazil visa' website
dead_ends.why_fails: Only the official e-visa portal issues a valid visa; third-party sites overcharge and may submit incorrectly.

workarounds.action: If you hold a US, Canadian, or Australian passport, apply for the Brazil e-visa on the official portal before travel.
workarounds.how: Apply via the official Brazil e-visa system well ahead of travel; carry the approved e-visa.

workarounds.action: Other nationalities should confirm visa-free eligibility and allowed days on the Brazilian government/consulate site.
workarounds.how: Check your specific passport's rule; many remain visa-free for up to 90 days.
